definition: BOJ and PBOC operate fundamentally different commodity-inflation mechanisms from Western central banks. BOJ: commodity inflation + anchored nominal rates = falling real rates = easier real conditions (the only major CB that welcomes commodity increases). PBOC: administered pricing (NDRC formula, strategic reserves, SOE loss absorption) partially decouples Chinese inflation from global commodity prices. This divergence amplifies the FX channel for other countries.
evidence: BOJ under YCC: commodity inflation reduces real rates — opposite of inflation-targeting frameworks
PBOC uses administrative tools (NDRC formula, strategic reserves, SOE loss absorption) not rates
Divergence (Fed/ECB tighten, BOJ accommodates, PBOC administers) widens rate differentials and strengthens dollar
Pair_3 debate called this the most valuable insight unique to rates_strategist_02
open_questions: Will BOJ normalize its policy framework post-YCC?
How long can PBOC administrative tools decouple domestic from global commodity prices?
Does the structural divergence increase or decrease global inflation volatility?
